text:
The countdown is on to the 2024 Olympics with just days until the opening ceremony in Paris, and excitement is reaching fever pitch after two global stars were spotted in the French capital.

Anticipation is building for the 2024 Olympics opening ceremony after two world-famous singers were seen in Paris.

Celine Dion was spotted checking into a luxurious hotel near the Arc de Triomphe amid reports she is expected to take the stage on Friday.

The ‘My Heart Will Go On’ singer will allegedly be paid a whopping $2 million to perform just one song, according to TMZ.

If she appears, it would be the 56-year-old’s first public performance in many years after being diagnosed with a rare neurological disease known as Stiff Person Syndrome.

It often causes muscle stiffness and painful spasms, with the Grammy-award winning singer shown suffering an intense seizure in her new documentary ‘I Am: Celine Dion’.

Lady Gaga was also seen smiling and waving at fans from the open sunroof of a car in the French capital, clad in a black jacket, hat and dark glasses.

The popstar has yet to share anything Olympics related on her social media, instead hinting new music is on the way in a brief Instagram post last week.

“Just me in the studio—happy as ever making music. Feel so grateful, heart is peaceful. It’s like meditation. I can’t wait for you to hear what I’m working on,” she wrote.

Hugely popular French-Malian singer Aya Nakamura is also rumoured to appear.

Despite the rampant speculation, the official list of entertainers booked to play for the athletes, politicians and VIPs at the opening ceremony remains under wraps.

For the first time in the history of the summer games, Friday’s ceremony will be held outside the athletics stadium.

Millions of viewers around the world will be treated to a colourful parade along the Seine River as the sun sets, as quays are transformed into spectator stands.

Athletes will float down the river on boats, rigged with cameras to capture all the action, before their arrive at the esplanade across the Eiffel Tower where all the usual traditions will be followed.

Pulling off such an ambitious spectacle will no doubt be a major artistic and security challenge.

French theatre director and actor Thomas Jolly, who is overseeing the opening ceremonies for both the Olympic and Paralympic Games, told AP heinitially felt “overwhelmed”by the task.

“I wondered how I could create a show where everyone can feel represented as part of this great union,” he said.

He also flagged he was keen to challenge some clichés about France.

“When we watch ‘Emily in Paris’ or ‘Amélie Poulain,’ we know it’s not quite the real Paris. We’re going to play with all those cliches, but we’re also going to challenge them,” he said.

“Paris is also a vibrant youth. Different cultures rubbing shoulders in the streets.”

Australians will have to get up bright and early on Saturday morning to catch the start of the opening ceremony in Paris, with live coverage from 3:00am AEST on Channel Nine.

title: Two legendary singers spotted in Paris days before 2024 Olympics opening ceremony kicks off along the Seine | Sky News Australia
